channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pakistani security forces battle to free about 300 hostages aboard a hijacked train
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Security forces battled hundreds of militants who were holding about 300 people hostage Wednesday on a train they had hijacked in remote southwestern Pakistan the day before, officials said. Militants wearing vests loaded with explosives surrounded the hostages.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Australia won't retaliate against 'unjustified' US tariffs on steel and aluminum
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says U.S. tariffs on Australian steel and aluminum are unjustified, but his government will not retaliate with its own tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Polls close in Greenland's capital for parliamentary election as Trump seeks control
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The polling station in Greenland’s capital closed Tuesday in a parliamentary election that will determine the leaders who will confront U.S. President Donald Trump’s effort to take control of the strategically placed Arctic country.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US resumes military aid and intelligence sharing as Ukraine says it is open to a 30-day ceasefire
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The Trump administration lifted its suspension of military aid and intelligence sharing for Ukraine, and Kyiv signaled that it was open to a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Russia, pending Moscow’s agreement, American and Ukrainian officials said Tuesday following talks
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK police arrest captain of cargo ship on suspicion of manslaughter over North Sea collision
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British police on Tuesday arrested the captain of a cargo ship on suspicion of manslaughter as they searched for answers about why it hit a tanker transporting jet fuel for the U.S. military off eastern England, setting both vessels ablaze.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario agrees to suspend electricity surcharge ahead of talks with US
item.description: The government of Ontario on Tuesday backed off its threat to impose a surcharge on electricity to three U.S. states amid broader trade talks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: US lifts military freeze on Ukraine in push for 30-day ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. will immediately restore military aid and intelligence sharing to Ukraine following bilateral talks in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, on Tuesday, with Ukraine expressing "readiness" to accept a 30-day ceasefire proposal. "Ukraine expressed readiness to accept the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: FBI joins Dominican Republic search for missing University of Pittsburgh student, police say
item.description: The FBI has joined the Dominican Republic’s police in searching for Sudiksha Konanki, the University of Pittsburgh student who went missing a week ago while on spring break. Konanki, 20, vanished Thursday in Punta Cana, a town located on the easternmost tip of the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Philippine ex-leader Duterte is being flown to The Hague to face charges of crimes against humanity
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police arrested former President Rodrigo Duterte in Manila on Tuesday and sent him by plane to the Netherlands to face charges of crimes against humanity at the International Criminal Court, President Ferdinand Marcos said.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Evangelical leader says US must protect Syrian Christians from attacks by jihadi terrorists
item.description: A senior evangelical leader has called on the U.S. to not recognize new Syrian regime following bloodshed of religious minorities, including Christians and Alawites, carried out by radical Islamists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump should be 'looking to reassure Americans' on economy
item.description: Former Fox News host Bill O'Reilly believes President Trump should be concerned about getting his economic message across as Americans face the rocky impact of his tariff policies and continued inflation, and that he should throw someone out there who's a calm and credible economist to soothe fears.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: US-Canada products are 99 percent tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, The Hill

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine will propose a limited ceasefire during talks with the US in Saudi Arabia, officials say
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — A Ukrainian delegation set to meet with America’s top diplomat in Saudi Arabia about ending the 3-year war with Russia will propose a ceasefire covering the Black Sea and long-range missile strikes, as well as the release of prisoners, two senior Ukrainian officials said
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Cargo ship hits a tanker in fiery crash off England
item.description: A cargo ship struck a tanker Monday in a fiery crash in the North Sea off the coast of eastern England, causing fuel to stream into local waters. MV Stena Immaculate, the tanker, carries oil and chemicals and features the U.S.’s flag.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Greek lawmaker attacks paintings in Athens' National Gallery, claiming they are offensive
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Greece's National Gallery said a Greek lawmaker on Monday attacked four paintings in an exhibition at the museum in Athens, including one he had previously criticized as offensive on religious grounds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis now 'out of danger from death' as health condition continues to improve
item.description: Vatican officials say that Pope Francis's medical condition has improved and that he is currently “out of danger from death," as of Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea fires missiles as US, South Korea begin their 1st joint military exercise of Trump's 2nd term
item.description: North Korea fired several ballistic missiles into the sea after South Korea and the U.S. kicked off their first major joint military exercise of President Donald Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis thanked volunteers Sunday for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his treatment for double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Romania blocks frontrunner from postponed presidential race
item.description: Protests have erupted in Romania after authorities barred Calin Georgescu, a candidate critical of NATO, from running in May's presidential election re-run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists vandalize Trump golf course in Scotland
item.description: In white bold-face capital letters, activists wrote “Gaza is not 4 sale” on Trump’s Turnberry golf course.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Mother of injured Hamas hostage directs plea to 'every mother in this world' to help secure son's release
item.description: Idit Ohel is fighting for the release of son Alon, who has been held hostage in Gaza since Oct. 7, 2023, and is at risk of going blind from an injury sustained in the Hamas attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump considering pressure on Russia and seeking deal with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump said he’s “strongly considering” sanctions and tariffs on Russia in hopes of forcing a settlement to the war in Ukraine. Trump also sent a letter to Iran’s leaders seeking to negotiate a nuclear deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the mi
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kremlin says a 2022 Ukrainian decree bans Zelenskyy from talks with Putin
item.description: Russia on Wednesday asked how Ukraine could attend potential talks on ending their three-year war when a Ukrainian decree from 2022 rules out negotiations with President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel’s cutoff of supplies to Gaza sends prices soaring as aid stockpiles dwindle
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s cutoff of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies to Gaza’s 2 million people has sent prices soaring and humanitarian groups into overdrive trying to distribute dwindling stocks to the most vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
